title: Too fast for me
desc: Ask for a repeat without losing face
setting: A colleague rattles off plans for a work outing at top speed. You catch maybe half.

steps.speaker: Kolleg
steps.es: Also mir träffed öis am halbi sächsi am Bahnhof bim Gleis drü, okay?
steps.en: So we meet at half past five at the station by platform three, okay?

options.es: Momänt, chasch das nomal säge? Du bisch mer z'schnäll gsii.
options.en: Hold on, can you say that again? You were too fast for me.
options.feedback: Honest, easy, no shame — and you'll actually get the details right.
You didn't, though — bluffing now means missing the meet-up later.
You repeat what you caught and ask for the rest — smart and natural.

steps.speaker: Kolleg
steps.es: Klar, sorry! Am halbi sächsi. Bim Gleis drü. Abgmacht?
steps.en: Sure, sorry! At half five. By platform three. Agreed?

options.es: Jetz han ich's. Halbi sächsi, Gleis drü. Merci!
options.en: Now I've got it. Half five, platform three. Thanks!
options.feedback: Confirming back the details — exactly how to lock a plan in.
Fair question, but confirm the plan first so it doesn't slip again.

steps.speaker: Kolleg
steps.es: Super. Übrigens redsch scho rächt guet, nur chli langsam no.
steps.en: Great. By the way you speak quite well now, just still a bit slow.

options.es: Langsam aber sicher, gäll. S'Schnälle chunnt no.
options.en: Slow but sure, eh. The speed will come.
options.feedback: Confident and good-humoured — the right way to take honest feedback.

options.es: Ich bi froh, verstahsch mi überhaupt.
options.en: I'm glad you understand me at all.
options.feedback: A touch self-deprecating — you're doing better than that, own it.
Playful blame is okay between colleagues, but keep it light.

steps.speaker: Kolleg
steps.es: Eba! Mir übed's am Fritig zäme bim Apéro.
steps.en: Exactly! We'll practise it together at the apéro on Friday.

options.es: Abgmacht! Denn lose ich der ganz gnau zue.
options.en: Deal! Then I'll listen to you very closely.
options.feedback: Warm and game — turning a stumble into a plan together. Lovely.
Easy and friendly — a solid close.
Lukewarm after a kind offer — a clearer yes keeps the goodwill.
